The right hand peaks at G#6 in measure 31; the left anchors from E1 at measure 1.

teachingGoals: Learn the two left-hand spans as the piece's skeleton: the 119-note line (measures 3-9) and the 156-note line (measures 20-26) each return twice, so two long passages unlock nearly the whole accompaniment.
Practice the 8-note right-hand cell in isolation, then spot its 51 appearances — at a 6.915 compression ratio it is the connective tissue of the entire right hand.

Build stamina for unbroken density: with no sounding measure below 24 onsets and no structural seam above 0.695 novelty, this file is one continuous paragraph — practice long spans, not bar-by-bar fragments.
Hear the harmony by ear against the flagged key mismatch: with zero labeled windows, listen for whether the bass's long lines pull toward the detected A center or the stated E, and let your ear rule.
styleTips: Lay the groove far back at 51 BPM — this style's pocket is a deep, late-sitting backbeat over an unhurried bass; listen for beats 2 and 4 landing a breath behind a pulse that never drags.
Play the dense texture in layers, not all at once: bring the two long bass lines forward, let the 8-note cell murmur, and listen for the middle voices blending into wash by design.
Treat the byte-identical closing echo (measures 38-42 returning at 44-48) as a fade in intensity — same notes, less insistence; listen for the second pass sounding like memory.
Imitate a reference recording by ear for the phrasing across the seamless form — with no structural seams stronger than 0.695, the records' breathing, not the barlines, should shape your phrases.
